title: Audrey Bergner and Aurelia Jeffery Traveling Across Lake Nahuel Huapi in Bariloche Rio Negro
description: Bariloche, Rio Negro: Audrey Bergner and Aurelia Jeffery traverse the serene waters of the Andes while resting against the backdrop of a mountain transit vessel. The soft, natural light filters through the transport windows, illuminating the intimate connection between a mother and her child during a long-distance excursion. Audrey wears a weathered cap, signaling a life of perpetual motion and exploration, while Aurelia finds comfort in the rhythmic stillness of the crossing. The interior of the cabin, with its functional upholstery and utilitarian design, captures the raw, unvarnished essence of transit within the Argentine Lake District. Every shadow and highlight on their faces reflects the shifting light of the Patagonian sky, emphasizing the tactile reality of traversing such a vast, remote landscape. This quiet moment of respite contrasts with the grand, dramatic scale of the surrounding mountain ranges and glacial lakes that define this frontier. The composition utilizes a close-cropped, candid perspective, prioritizing the emotional narrative over the vast, external geography of the crossing. Documenting the quiet, domestic side of travel provides a profound understanding of how families navigate and find belonging in the rugged environments of South America. This image serves as a testament to the resilience and grace found in the shared experiences of nomadic life. This image is a collaborative production by Samuel Jeffery and Audrey Bergner for Project 23.
